mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) relative to baseline during rate cuts, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts yields negative 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of instances, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero within 12 months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading below 2.0% confirms disinflation that allows equity markets to price in lower future discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 5-day returns in > 60% of low-inflation readings, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates signals structural labor market deterioration that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence yields positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly increases corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during post-shock recovery phases indicate fragile supply chains that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops during recovery phases produce positive 5-day SPX returns in > 62% of instances, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.
